Idiom (Cham): Havar la kapo inter la nubi
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verbal phrase (verb + definite noun phrase + prepositional phrase); metaphorical spatial construction
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be distracted, unrealistic, or lost in impractical dreams
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Esar tro sonjemanta o ne realista
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, workplace critique, education, humor
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Inspired by early 20th-century European aviation imagery and modernist satire in Ido-speaking circles
First Example Sentence: Il sempre havas la kapo inter la nubi dum la reuniono, ed il forgesas la detalji.
Second Example Sentence: Ne havas la kapo inter la nubi, ni bezonas praktika solvo.
Third Example Sentence: La studanto havis la kapo inter la nubi ed ne audis la questiono.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Transitive verb phrase with direct object + prepositional phrase; causative metaphor
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To aggravate a conflict or intensify a problem
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from Mediterranean culinary practices; metaphor adopted into Ido reformist discourse
First Example Sentence: Lia komento nur metas oleo sur la fajro.
Second Example Sentence: Ne metas oleo sur la fajro dum la debato.
Third Example Sentence: La gazetisto metis oleo sur la fajro per sensacionala titulo.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Copular construction with prepositional complement; binary pressure metaphor
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be caught between two opposing pressures
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Blacksmith imagery common in European industrial narratives influencing early Ido publications
First Example Sentence: Me esas inter martelo e amboso inter mea chefo e la kliento.
Second Example Sentence: Li sentis su inter martelo e amboso dum la negocado.
Third Example Sentence: La ministro esas inter martelo e amboso pro la opoziciono.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Transitive verb phrase; bodily harm metaphor for deception
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To deceive someone openly or shamelessly
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Satirical idiom appearing in early Ido reformist pamphlets criticizing manipulation
First Example Sentence: Vu ne povas pikar mea okuli per tal argumento.
Second Example Sentence: La komercisto pikis la okuli di la turistoj.
Third Example Sentence: Il provas pikar nia okuli per falsi statistiki.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Causative verb phrase; hyperbolic transformation metaphor
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To exaggerate a minor issue into something huge
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Shared European hyperbole adapted into Ido vernacular usage
First Example Sentence: Ne facez el musho elefanto pro tal eroro.
Second Example Sentence: Ili facis el musho elefanto dum la diskuto.
Third Example Sentence: La gazetaro facas el musho elefanto por vendigar pluse.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To desire something strongly or feel envious
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Medieval European imagery of beasts baring teeth symbolizing desire
First Example Sentence: Me havas longa denti por ta nova auto.
Second Example Sentence: Ili havas longa denti vidante la premii.
Third Example Sentence: La infanti havis longa denti por la dolci.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Transitive verb phrase; metaphor of thread continuity
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To lose track of reasoning or narrative flow
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Allusion to classical myth of Ariadne’s thread, widely referenced in European education
First Example Sentence: Pardonez, me perdis la filo dum mea expliko.
Second Example Sentence: Il ofte perdas la filo en longa paroladi.
Third Example Sentence: Ni ne devas perdar la filo di la argumento.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Intransitive verb phrase; classical symbolic metaphor
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Esar tro kontenta pro pasinta suceso
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reference to Roman laurel wreaths awarded to victors; adopted in reformist literature
First Example Sentence: Ne dormez sur la lauri pos vua triumfo.
Second Example Sentence: La kompanio dormis sur la lauri ed perdis merkato.
Third Example Sentence: Politikisti qui dormas sur la lauri riskas elektiona perdo.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be completely satisfied or fed up
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in agrarian food-centered expressions common across European communities
First Example Sentence: Me havas la ventro plena de tua plendi.
Second Example Sentence: Post la festo, ni havis la ventro plena.
Third Example Sentence: El havas la ventro plena de politika promisi.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Transitive verb phrase with numeral object; hunting metaphor
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To attempt two goals simultaneously and risk failing both
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Probar realigar du taski samtempe kun risko di fracaso
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Hunting proverb common in Eastern European folklore influencing Ido speakers
First Example Sentence: Se tu chasas du lepore samtempe, tu kaptos nulo.
Second Example Sentence: El chasis du lepore samtempe ed perdis ambi oportuni.
Third Example Sentence: Ni ne chasu du lepore samtempe dum la projekto.
